witnesses.description: The base case for the finite prefix-sum formula.
A counterexample showing that the property n = 0 has a base case but fails the induction step.

checks.claim: The prefix-sum formula holds at n = 0.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes sum(0..0) and 0*(0+1)/2 exactly.

checks.claim: There exists k <= 8 such that P(k) holds for the prefix-sum formula but P(k + 1) fails.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates k = 0..8 and finds no bounded step counterexample; the Axeyum regression checks the final bad_step_count = 0 versus bad_step_count >= 1 contradiction through checked QF_LIA arithmetic evidence.

checks.claim: There exists n <= 9 where the prefix-sum formula fails.
checks.notes: The validator enumerates n = 0..9 and finds no bounded formula failure.

checks.claim: The candidate property n = 0 has a bounded induction-step counterexample.
checks.notes: The validator checks P(0) is true and P(1) is false for the candidate property n = 0.

checks.claim: The full natural-number induction schema turns checked base and step obligations into a theorem for all n.

data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependency

checks.notes: This row is theorem-prover metadata; the finite obligations do not themselves prove the universal schema.
